identity.name: DeepBookV3
identity.oneLiner: Sui's next-generation decentralized central limit order book (CLOB) for low-latency on-chain trading.

sources.quote: DeepBookV3 is a next-generation decentralized central limit order book (CLOB) built on Sui.

entities.name: DeepBookV3
entities.description: A next-generation decentralized central limit order book (CLOB) built on Sui.

sources.quote: DeepBookV3 is a next-generation decentralized central limit order book (CLOB) built on Sui.

entities.name: Sui
entities.description: The Layer-1 blockchain whose parallel execution and low fees DeepBookV3 is built on.

sources.quote: DeepBookV3 leverages Sui's parallel execution and low transaction fees to bring a highly performant, low-latency exchange on chain.

entities.name: DEEP token
entities.description: The token that pays for DeepBookV3 trading fees and unlocks staking incentives.

sources.quote: The DEEP token pays for trading fees on the exchange.

entities.name: Central limit order book (CLOB)
entities.description: An on-chain order book that logs bids and asks in chronological order and matches the two sides.

sources.quote: As a CLOB, DeepBookV3 works like a digital ledger, logging bids and asks in chronological order and automatically finding matches between the two sides.

entities.name: Pool
entities.description: A shared object that represents one market and manages its order book, users, and stakes.

sources.quote: A shared object that represents one market and is responsible for managing its order book, users, stakes, and so on.

entities.name: BalanceManager
entities.description: A shared object that holds all balances of a single account, with one owner and up to 1000 traders.

sources.quote: The `BalanceManager` is a shared object that holds all balances of a single account. It has one owner and can have up to 1000 traders.

entities.name: Flash loans
entities.description: Uncollateralized loans borrowed and repaid within the same programmable transaction block.

sources.quote: Flash loans by definition are uncollateralized loans that are borrowed and repaid within the same programmable transaction block.

entities.name: Governance & staking
entities.description: Stakers can propose taker fee, maker fee, and stake-required parameter changes each epoch.

sources.quote: Every epoch, users with nonzero stake can submit a proposal to change these parameters.

entities.name: Matching engine
entities.description: DeepBookV3 enhances the existing matching engine that pairs bids with asks.

sources.quote: enhancements to the existing matching engine.

entities.name: Swaps
entities.description: An AMM-style swap interface callable directly with Coin objects without a BalanceManager.

sources.quote: DeepBook provides a swap-like interface commonly seen in automatic market makers (AMMs).

entities.name: BigVector
entities.description: An onchain B+ Tree data structure backing the order book with near-constant-time access.

sources.quote: `BigVector` is an arbitrary sized vector-like data structure, implemented using an onchain B+ Tree to support almost constant time (log base max_fan_out) random access, insertion and removal.

entities.name: MystenLabs
entities.description: The organization that owns the DeepBookV3 source repository.

sources.quote: org="MystenLabs" repo="deepbookv3"

governance determines the fee for paying in DEEP tokens, which is 20% lower than the fee for using input tokens.
It has one owner and can have up to 1000 traders.
This object holds up to 100 data points representing the conversion rate between the pool's base or quote asset and DEEP.

Whitelisted pools have 0% trading fees.
DeepBookV3 is a next-generation decentralized central limit order book (CLOB) built on Sui.
The latest version delivers new features including flash loans, governance, improved account abstraction, and enhancements to the existing matching engine.
DeepBook provides a swap-like interface commonly seen in automatic market makers (AMMs).
The DEEP token pays for trading fees on the exchange.
A shared object that represents one market and is responsible for managing its order book, users, stakes, and so on.
Used to source a user's funds when placing orders. A single `BalanceManager` can be used between all pools.
The `Book` struct maintains two `BigVector<Order>` objects for bids and asks, as well as some metadata.
stores `Governance`, `History`, and `Account`.
DeepBookV3's CLOB architecture enables you to enter market and limit orders.
org="MystenLabs" repo="deepbookv3"
enhancements to the existing matching engine.
Stake required represents the amount of DEEP tokens that a user must have staked in this specific pool to be eligible for taker and maker incentives.

questions.question: What is DeepBookV3?
questions.answer: DeepBookV3 is a next-generation decentralized central limit order book (CLOB) built on Sui, leveraging Sui's parallel execution and low transaction fees for a highly performant, low-latency on-chain exchange.

sources.quote: DeepBookV3 is a next-generation decentralized central limit order book (CLOB) built on Sui.

questions.question: How is DeepBookV3 designed architecturally?
questions.answer: The design revolves around three shared objects: the Pool (one market), the PoolRegistry (prevents duplicate pools), and the BalanceManager (sources user funds across pools).

sources.quote: the DeepBookV3 design follows the following flow, which revolves around three shared objects:

questions.question: How much are DeepBookV3 trading fees?
questions.answer: Paying in DEEP is 20% lower than paying in input tokens, and staked takers can cut fees by half to as low as 0.25 bps on stable pairs and 2.5 bps on volatile pairs.

sources.quote: governance determines the fee for paying in DEEP tokens, which is 20% lower than the fee for using input tokens.

questions.question: What new features does DeepBookV3 add?
questions.answer: DeepBookV3 delivers flash loans, governance, improved account abstraction, and enhancements to the existing matching engine.

sources.quote: The latest version delivers new features including flash loans, governance, improved account abstraction, and enhancements to the existing matching engine.

questions.question: What is a BalanceManager?
questions.answer: A BalanceManager is a shared object that holds all balances of a single account; it has one owner and can have up to 1000 traders, and a single one can be used across all pools.

sources.quote: The `BalanceManager` is a shared object that holds all balances of a single account. It has one owner and can have up to 1000 traders.

questions.question: How do flash loans work in DeepBookV3?
questions.answer: Flash loans are uncollateralized loans borrowed and repaid within the same programmable transaction block; they return a FlashLoan hot potato that must be returned by the end of the call, and the whole atomic transaction fails otherwise.

sources.quote: Flash loans return a `FlashLoan` hot potato (struct with no abilities), which must be returned back to the pool by the end of the call.

questions.question: Who is DeepBookV3 for?
questions.answer: It has no end-user trading interface; instead it offers built-in trading functionality that can support token trades from decentralized exchanges, wallets, or other apps, lowering the barrier for active market makers.

sources.quote: it offers built-in trading functionality that can support token trades from decentralized exchanges, wallets, or other apps.

questions.question: How does DeepBookV3 governance work?
questions.answer: Every epoch, users with nonzero stake can submit proposals to change the taker fee, maker fee, and stake required; quorum is half of total voting power, with the voting power cutoff currently set to 100,000 DEEP.

sources.quote: Every epoch, users with nonzero stake can submit a proposal to change these parameters.

questions.question: How does DeepBookV3 ensure transparency?
questions.answer: As a CLOB it works like a digital ledger that logs bids and asks chronologically; the ledger is open so people can view trades and prices, giving clear proof of fairness.

sources.quote: The digital ledger is open so people can view the trades and prices, giving clear proof of fairness.
